channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: 4 Major Grant Opportunities for NGOs, Civil Society Organizations, Social Enterprises, and Healthcare Organizations in 2026
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Organizations working to create positive social, environmental, and health impact now have access to several outstanding funding opportunities designed to strengthen their programs, expand their reach, and improve sustainability. Whether you are a nonprofit, civil society organization, community-based organization (CBO), social enterprise, or healthcare institution, these grants provide financial resources, strategic partnerships, and capacity-building support […]

The post 4 Major Grant Opportunities for NGOs, Civil Society Organizations, Social Enterprises, and Healthcare Organizations in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working to create positive social, environmental, and health impact now have access to several outstanding funding opportunities designed to strengthen their programs, expand their reach, and improve sustainability. Whether you are a nonprofit, civil society organization, community-based organization (CBO), social enterprise, or healthcare institution, these grants provide financial resources, strategic partnerships, and capacity-building support to help maximize your impact.

This article highlights four major grant opportunities currently available for organizations across Africa, Sub-Saharan Africa, low- and middle-income countries, and other eligible regions. These opportunities are offered by respected international organizations committed to supporting locally led development, social innovation, environmental protection, and healthcare quality improvement.

The funding opportunities featured include:

  • Oak Foundation Multi-Year Grants
  • New Africa Fund Matching Grants
  • Segal Family Foundation Partnership Funding
  • ISQua Quality Improvement Grants

Below is everything you need to know about each opportunity.

 

1. Oak Foundation Multi-Year Grants (Rolling Applications)

The Oak Foundation provides substantial multi-year grants to nonprofit organizations and civil society organizations working to achieve long-term social and environmental change.

Unlike many grant programs, organizations do not necessarily need to receive an invitation before expressing their interest. Eligible organizations whose work aligns closely with the Foundation’s priorities may submit an unsolicited Letter of Inquiry (LOI) for consideration.

Oak Foundation is known for supporting initiatives that create sustainable, systemic, and long-lasting impact rather than short-term interventions.

Funding Available

Organizations may receive:

  • Average grant size of approximately USD $900,000
  • Funding awards of up to USD $10 million for exceptional initiatives

The Foundation primarily supports organizations capable of delivering significant impact in areas aligned with its funding priorities.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible applicants include:

  • Nonprofit organizations
  • Civil society organizations

Applicants should demonstrate strong alignment with the Oak Foundation’s funding priorities.

Deadline

Rolling applications (No fixed deadline)

Organizations may submit a Letter of Inquiry throughout the year.

 

2. New Africa Fund Matching Grants 

The New Africa Fund is supporting early-stage organizations across Africa by providing financial assistance together with organizational development support.

The initiative aims to strengthen the long-term sustainability of African organizations by combining matching grants with fiscal sponsorship and capacity-building opportunities.

Funding Available

Successful organizations may receive:

  • Up to USD $5,000 in matching funds

Additional Benefits

Selected organizations will also benefit from:

  • Twelve (12) months of free fiscal sponsorship
  • Capacity-building opportunities
  • Access to organizational development support
  • Opportunity for top-performing organizations to attend an intensive workshop in Kigali, Rwanda

These additional services are designed to help organizations strengthen governance, fundraising capacity, and operational sustainability.

Eligible Applicants

Applications are welcomed from:

  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Community-Based Organizations (CBOs)
  • Social enterprises

Organizations must be located within one of the 55 African Union Member States.

Deadline

14 August 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline.

 

3. Segal Family Foundation Partnership Funding

The Segal Family Foundation supports locally led organizations that are driving sustainable development across Sub-Saharan Africa.

Rather than operating through a traditional open grant competition, the Foundation builds long-term partnerships with organizations demonstrating exceptional impact and leadership.

Its funding approach emphasizes trust-based philanthropy by providing flexible, unrestricted support that allows organizations to invest where resources are most needed.

Funding Support

Partner organizations receive:

  • Flexible unrestricted funding
  • Long-term strategic partnership
  • Organizational strengthening support
  • Capacity development
  • Relationship-based collaboration

Because the funding is unrestricted, organizations have greater flexibility to allocate resources according to their strategic priorities.

Who Can Apply?

The Foundation works with:

  • High-impact organizations
  • Locally led development organizations
  • Organizations operating across Sub-Saharan Africa

Application Process

There is no open call for applications.

Instead, new partners are identified through trusted referral networks and strategic recommendations.

Organizations interested in learning more can review the Foundation’s partnership approach through its official website.

 

4. ISQua Quality Improvement Grant 

The International Society for Quality in Health Care (ISQua) is inviting applications from organizations committed to improving healthcare quality and patient safety in low- and middle-income countries.

The grant supports initiatives that strengthen healthcare systems and improve the delivery of safe, high-quality healthcare services.

Funding Available

Successful applicants may receive:

  • €15,000

Eligible Organizations

The grant is open to:

  • Healthcare organizations
  • Hospitals
  • Professional associations
  • Health-focused NGOs

Applicants should be based in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs).

Deadline

12 August 2026

Interested organizations should complete their applications before the closing date.

 

Comparison of the Four Funding Opportunities

Opportunity Funding Eligible Applicants Deadline
Oak Foundation Average USD $900,000 (up to USD $10 million) Nonprofits and civil society organizations Rolling
New Africa Fund Up to USD $5,000 + fiscal sponsorship NGOs, CBOs, Social Enterprises in Africa 14 August 2026
Segal Family Foundation Flexible unrestricted funding High-impact organizations in Sub-Saharan Africa No open call
ISQua €15,000 Healthcare organizations and health NGOs in LMICs 12 August 2026

 

Why Organizations Should Consider These Opportunities

Each of these funding opportunities supports organizations seeking to increase their impact while strengthening institutional capacity.

Depending on your organization’s mission, these opportunities can provide:

  • Significant grant funding
  • Flexible unrestricted financial support
  • Long-term strategic partnerships
  • Capacity-building opportunities
  • Fiscal sponsorship
  • Professional networking
  • Healthcare quality improvement funding
  • Organizational sustainability support
  • Access to international development partners

Whether your organization focuses on education, health, community development, environmental protection, social innovation, or entrepreneurship, one of these opportunities may align with your goals.

Organizations are encouraged to carefully review the eligibility requirements and application procedures for each fund and prepare strong proposals well in advance of the applicable deadlines.

 

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Grant opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post 4 Major Grant Opportunities for NGOs, Civil Society Organizations, Social Enterprises, and Healthcare Organizations in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNESCO International Review of Education Calls for Reviewers 2026: Join a Global Network of Scholars in Lifelong Learning (Apply by 31 August 2026)
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The International Review of Education – Journal of Lifelong Learning (IRE), one of the world’s most respected academic journals in comparative education, has officially opened a Call for Reviewers. Scholars with expertise in lifelong learning and related disciplines are invited to express their interest in joining the journal’s international reviewer community. Published under the auspices […]

The post UNESCO International Review of Education Calls for Reviewers 2026: Join a Global Network of Scholars in Lifelong Learning (Apply by 31 August 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Review of Education – Journal of Lifelong Learning (IRE), one of the world’s most respected academic journals in comparative education, has officially opened a Call for Reviewers. Scholars with expertise in lifelong learning and related disciplines are invited to express their interest in joining the journal’s international reviewer community.

Published under the auspices of UNESCO since 1955 and managed by the UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning (UIL) in Hamburg, Germany, the journal is seeking both emerging and experienced researchers who are passionate about advancing global education research.

If you have academic expertise in lifelong learning, adult education, education policy, technology in education, or other related fields, this is an excellent opportunity to contribute to high-quality international scholarship while expanding your professional network.

Application Deadline: 31 August 2026

 

About the International Review of Education (IRE)

Founded in 1931, the International Review of Education (IRE) is the longest-running international journal of comparative education in the world.

Since 1955, the journal has operated under the aegis of UNESCO, making it one of the leading international platforms for research and policy discussions related to education and lifelong learning.

The journal is managed by the editorial team at the UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning (UIL) in Hamburg, Germany, under the guidance of an international editorial board.

IRE publishes scholarly work that contributes to global understanding of lifelong learning through:

  • Empirical research
  • Theoretical and conceptual studies
  • Policy analyses
  • Comparative education research
  • Interdisciplinary scholarship

The journal welcomes both:

  • Qualitative research
  • Quantitative research

Submissions are accepted in:

  • English
  • French

Importantly, the journal encourages scholarship from all regions of the world, ensuring that diverse educational perspectives are represented.

 

Why UNESCO Is Expanding Its Reviewer Pool

High-quality academic publishing depends on knowledgeable reviewers who evaluate submitted manuscripts and provide constructive feedback to authors and editors.

To strengthen this important process, UNESCO is expanding its international pool of reviewers.

The organization is inviting:

  • Early-career scholars
  • Emerging researchers
  • Established academics
  • Scholar-practitioners

who work within the broad field of lifelong learning and related disciplines.

Reviewers play a vital role in maintaining the journal’s academic excellence while helping shape research that informs education policy and practice worldwide.

 

Areas of Expertise Being Sought

UNESCO is particularly interested in reviewers with expertise in one or more of the following areas:

  1. Lifelong learning policy and practice
  2. Adult education
  3. Adult and family literacy
  4. Post-literacy education
  5. Learning in non-formal settings
  6. Learning in informal settings
  7. Open learning
  8. Distance learning
  9. Education for disadvantaged learners
  10. Education for vulnerable learners
  11. Vocational education and training (TVET)
  12. Workplace learning
  13. Human rights
  14. Community education
  15. Popular education
  16. Indigenous education
  17. Indigenous knowledge systems
  18. Decolonisation of education
  19. Citizenship education
  20. Education in conflict settings
  21. Education in post-conflict settings
  22. Education and technology

Scholars with interdisciplinary expertise are especially encouraged to apply.

 

UNESCO Encourages Diverse Global Participation

One of the defining characteristics of the International Review of Education is its strong representation of researchers and scholar-practitioners from the Global South.

UNESCO therefore encourages applications from:

  • Scholars from diverse geographic regions
  • Underrepresented groups
  • Researchers from developing countries
  • Academics from both established and emerging institutions

The journal aims to build an inclusive reviewer community that reflects the diversity of educational experiences around the world.

 

Responsibilities of Reviewers

Selected reviewers will contribute to the journal by reviewing approximately:

  • 2 to 4 manuscripts each year

Reviewers will:

  • Evaluate submitted manuscripts
  • Provide constructive feedback
  • Support authors in improving their work
  • Help editors make publication decisions
  • Maintain high academic standards

Serving as a reviewer also provides opportunities to engage more closely with one of UNESCO’s flagship education journals.

 

Opportunities for Professional Growth

Joining the IRE reviewer community offers numerous professional benefits, including:

  • Contributing to international education scholarship
  • Expanding your academic network
  • Staying current with cutting-edge research before publication
  • Strengthening your peer-review experience
  • Supporting evidence-based education policy
  • Collaborating with an international community of researchers

UNESCO also notes that active reviewers may have the prospect of joining the journal’s pool of consulting editors and/or editorial board in the future.

 

How to Apply

Interested scholars should submit:

  • A Statement of Interest
  • A list of the main subject areas they wish to review
  • An up-to-date Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Applications should be sent via email to:

ire@unesco.org

 

Application Deadline

Deadline: 31 August 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their materials before the deadline to be considered for inclusion in UNESCO’s expanding reviewer community.

 

Why You Should Apply

Serving as an IRE reviewer allows scholars to contribute meaningfully to global education research while gaining valuable professional recognition.

Whether you are an emerging researcher seeking to build your academic profile or an experienced scholar wishing to contribute your expertise, becoming an IRE reviewer provides an excellent opportunity to support high-quality research on lifelong learning across the globe.

By participating in the peer-review process, you will help shape future discussions on education policy, lifelong learning, inclusion, technology, literacy, and educational innovation worldwide.

For scholars committed to advancing education research, this UNESCO opportunity represents an important avenue for international engagement and academic service.

If you are interested, please submit your statement of interest (including a list of the main areas in which you would be interested in reviewing) and your CV to the IRE Editorial Team at ire@unesco.org by 31 August 2026.

 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here 

The post UNESCO International Review of Education Calls for Reviewers 2026: Join a Global Network of Scholars in Lifelong Learning (Apply by 31 August 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GrowthAfrica Jobs 2026: Apply for Innovation, Gender, and M&E Roles Across Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, and Ghana
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
GrowthAfrica is recruiting passionate, purpose-driven professionals to join its expanding team and support the growth of entrepreneurship and innovation across Africa. With multiple positions currently open, the organization is seeking talented individuals who are committed to empowering entrepreneurs, strengthening innovation ecosystems, and driving sustainable economic development across the continent. The available roles span several key […]

The post GrowthAfrica Jobs 2026: Apply for Innovation, Gender, and M&E Roles Across Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, and Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
GrowthAfrica is recruiting passionate, purpose-driven professionals to join its expanding team and support the growth of entrepreneurship and innovation across Africa. With multiple positions currently open, the organization is seeking talented individuals who are committed to empowering entrepreneurs, strengthening innovation ecosystems, and driving sustainable economic development across the continent.

The available roles span several key functions, including innovation management, entrepreneurship support, gender mainstreaming, safeguarding, and monitoring and evaluation (M&E). These opportunities are based within a multi-country entrepreneurship programme operating in Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, and Ghana.

Unlike many recruitment drives, these positions do not have a fixed application deadline. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis, and GrowthAfrica will continue reviewing candidates until the vacancies are filled. Interested applicants are therefore encouraged to apply as early as possible.

 

About GrowthAfrica

GrowthAfrica is an organization dedicated to accelerating ventures in and into Africa by supporting entrepreneurs, startups, investors, and organizations seeking to create sustainable business growth across the continent.

The organization works closely with entrepreneurs, partners, investors, and development stakeholders to facilitate business development, innovation, and enterprise growth. Through its entrepreneurship programmes, advisory services, and investment support, GrowthAfrica helps businesses seize opportunities and scale their impact across African markets.

Beyond supporting entrepreneurs, GrowthAfrica is equally committed to developing its own workforce by providing an environment where employees can continuously learn, grow professionally, and contribute meaningfully to impactful initiatives.

The organization describes itself as one that values passion, drive, creativity, innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning.

 

Why Work at GrowthAfrica?

GrowthAfrica emphasizes that working with the organization is much more than simply securing employment. Employees become part of a mission-driven team focused on accelerating entrepreneurship and economic transformation across Africa.

Successful candidates can expect:

  • A dynamic international working environment.
  • Opportunities to contribute to entrepreneurship development across multiple African countries.
  • Continuous professional learning and personal development.
  • Exposure to innovation ecosystems and startup support programmes.
  • Collaboration with young, energetic, and diverse teams.
  • Opportunities to strengthen leadership and technical expertise.
  • Meaningful work that contributes to Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem.

The organization encourages team members to actively invest in their own professional growth while providing the necessary support and learning environment.

 

Current Job Openings

GrowthAfrica is currently recruiting for the following positions.

1. Innovation Lead

The Innovation Lead will oversee the innovation journey for one of GrowthAfrica’s flagship entrepreneurship programmes.

The successful candidate will:

  • Design innovation methodologies.
  • Strengthen programme quality.
  • Coach country teams.
  • Support entrepreneurs in transforming ideas into viable businesses.
  • Coordinate innovation activities across Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, and Ghana.

This role is ideal for professionals experienced in innovation management, entrepreneurship development, programme design, and leadership.

 

2. Innovation Catalyst

The Innovation Catalyst will work directly with entrepreneurs throughout the innovation process.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Facilitating innovation workshops.
  • Coaching entrepreneurs during ideation.
  • Supporting prototype development.
  • Helping participants validate business ideas.
  • Building partnerships that strengthen local innovation ecosystems.

The role is available within entrepreneurship programmes operating across Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, or Ghana.

Candidates with experience in startup incubation, entrepreneurship support, innovation facilitation, or business development are encouraged to apply.

 

3. Gender Mainstreaming & Safeguarding Associate

GrowthAfrica is also recruiting a Gender Mainstreaming & Safeguarding Associate to help strengthen inclusive entrepreneurship programming.

Responsibilities include:

  • Promoting gender equality.
  • Supporting safeguarding initiatives.
  • Creating safe and inclusive learning environments.
  • Strengthening participant protection.
  • Improving programme design through inclusive practices.
  • Supporting organizational safeguarding policies.

This position contributes to programme implementation across four African countries and is suited to professionals experienced in gender, social inclusion, safeguarding, or development programming.

 

4. M&E Data Coordinator

The M&E Data Coordinator will lead monitoring, evaluation, and learning activities for the entrepreneurship programme.

Core responsibilities include:

  • Building monitoring and evaluation systems.
  • Managing programme data.
  • Generating evidence for programme improvement.
  • Producing data-driven insights.
  • Supporting organizational learning.
  • Demonstrating programme impact.
  • Informing decision-making across Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, and Ghana.

Professionals with expertise in monitoring and evaluation, data management, programme performance, and impact measurement are encouraged to apply.

 

Internship Opportunities

In addition to full-time employment, GrowthAfrica also offers internship opportunities for students interested in gaining international experience.

Internships are designed for:

  • Open-minded students.
  • Creative individuals.
  • Proactive learners.
  • Students seeking international work experience.
  • Individuals interested in entrepreneurship and innovation.

Interns benefit from working alongside an energetic international team while gaining practical experience supporting entrepreneurship programmes across Africa.

 

Projects and Thesis Opportunities

GrowthAfrica welcomes applications from individuals interested in research projects or academic theses.

Applicants may:

  • Apply for available project opportunities.
  • Submit their own research ideas.
  • Propose innovative methodologies.
  • Suggest new tools and systems.
  • Contribute ideas that improve organizational processes.

The organization actively seeks fresh insights that can strengthen its programmes and operations.

 

Application Requirements

Candidates applying for positions at GrowthAfrica should prepare the following documents:

  • An up-to-date Curriculum Vitae (CV).
  • A tailored cover letter specific to the position.
  • Other relevant supporting documents, where applicable.

Applicants should ensure that all documents clearly demonstrate how their experience and qualifications align with the advertised role.

 

Application Guidelines

GrowthAfrica requests applicants to follow its recruitment instructions carefully to ensure efficient processing of applications.

Applicants should note the following:

Required Documentation

Applicants should submit:

  • CV
  • Tailored cover letter
  • Relevant supporting documents

Attachment Requirements

  • Maximum attachment size: 5 MB
  • Do not upload zipped files.

How to Apply

To submit an application:

  1. Visit the GrowthAfrica careers page.
  2. Navigate to Current Openings.
  3. Select the desired position.
  4. Click Apply.
  5. Upload the required documents.

Failure to follow the application instructions may result in the application not being considered.

 

Rolling Recruitment Process

GrowthAfrica has confirmed that all advertised positions remain open without a specified deadline.

Recruitment is conducted on a rolling basis, meaning applications are reviewed continuously until suitable candidates are identified and the positions are filled.

Prospective applicants are therefore encouraged to submit their applications as soon as possible to maximize their chances of consideration.

 

Why You Should Apply

GrowthAfrica offers professionals the opportunity to contribute to one of Africa’s leading entrepreneurship support organizations while working across multiple countries and innovation ecosystems.

Whether your expertise lies in innovation, entrepreneurship, monitoring and evaluation, safeguarding, gender inclusion, or programme management, these roles provide an opportunity to help shape the future of African entrepreneurship while developing your own professional career.

With an emphasis on learning, collaboration, and impact, GrowthAfrica continues to attract professionals who are passionate about creating sustainable business growth and empowering entrepreneurs across the continent.

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post GrowthAfrica Jobs 2026: Apply for Innovation, Gender, and M&E Roles Across Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, and Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Artivism Governance Fellow 2026: Fully Remote Global Fellowship Prioritizing Global South Leaders | Earn USD 1,200–1,800 Monthly While Shaping Democratic Governance for a Global Arts and Social Justice Movement
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Global Artivism is inviting expressions of interest for its Governance Fellow 2026, a unique remote fellowship opportunity for emerging governance professionals, community organizers, movement builders, researchers, and civic leaders who are passionate about democratic governance, institutional design, and social justice. The fellowship offers an opportunity to contribute to one of the world’s leading Global Majority-led […]

The post Global Artivism Governance Fellow 2026: Fully Remote Global Fellowship Prioritizing Global South Leaders | Earn USD 1,200–1,800 Monthly While Shaping Democratic Governance for a Global Arts and Social Justice Movement appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Global Artivism is inviting expressions of interest for its Governance Fellow 2026, a unique remote fellowship opportunity for emerging governance professionals, community organizers, movement builders, researchers, and civic leaders who are passionate about democratic governance, institutional design, and social justice. The fellowship offers an opportunity to contribute to one of the world’s leading Global Majority-led movements that leverages arts and culture as powerful tools for social transformation.

This 12-month independent contractor fellowship is open globally, with priority given to applicants from the Global South. Fellows will work remotely while collaborating with an international network of artists, activists, governance experts, and movement leaders dedicated to building a more just, equitable, and sustainable world.

The Governance Fellow will support the development of governance systems, democratic institutional processes, committee coordination, and strategic documentation that strengthens Global Artivism’s organizational infrastructure during an important phase of its democratic transition.

Application Deadline: 16 July 2026 (10:00 PM MYT / UTC+8).

About Global Artivism

Global Artivism is a Global Majority-led international movement committed to advancing justice, ecological integrity, and collective liberation through the transformative power of arts and culture.

Rather than viewing art simply as creative expression, Global Artivism embraces art as praxis—a practical force capable of:

  • Mobilizing communities
  • Influencing public narratives
  • Challenging injustice
  • Building solidarity
  • Transforming systems
  • Advancing climate justice
  • Promoting social equity
  • Supporting democratic participation

The movement intentionally centers leadership from communities most affected by injustice, particularly across the Global South, ensuring that its governance reflects the realities and lived experiences of the majority of the world’s population.

Global Artivism connects:

  • Artists
  • Cultural workers
  • Human rights advocates
  • Community organizers
  • Researchers
  • Social justice activists
  • Environmental defenders
  • Movement builders

Its work focuses on strengthening collaborative infrastructure that enables long-term, sustainable social change.

Core Principles of Global Artivism

The Governance Fellow will contribute to an organization grounded in several guiding principles:

  • Global Majority leadership
  • South-South solidarity
  • Distributed leadership
  • Shared decision-making
  • Artists at the center of social transformation
  • Community-centered governance
  • Intersectionality
  • Transparency
  • Accountability
  • Trust-based collaboration
  • Emergent leadership

These principles influence how decisions are made, how governance structures evolve, and how collaboration occurs across the movement.

About the Governance Fellow Position

The Governance Fellow engagement is designed as a structured, mentored opportunity that provides hands-on experience supporting governance systems within an international movement organization.

The fellowship focuses on:

  • Governance architecture
  • Democratic institutional design
  • Committee coordination
  • Governance research
  • Documentation systems
  • Electoral process development
  • Organizational learning

The successful fellow will work closely with the Governance Lead while collaborating with members of the Movement Secretariat, Global Council, and governance committees.

Fellowship Duration

The engagement includes:

  • 12-month contract
  • Starting in August 2026
  • Possibility of renewal depending on funding availability and mutual agreement

The position is fully remote and flexible, although fellows are expected to participate in scheduled meetings across multiple international time zones.

Remote Work Arrangement

The Governance Fellow position offers considerable flexibility.

Contractors may:

  • Set their own working schedule
  • Choose their preferred working methods
  • Work remotely from anywhere globally
  • Continue providing services to other organizations or clients

However, participants should be available for scheduled collaborative meetings involving colleagues across different regions and time zones.

Key Responsibilities

The Governance Fellow will contribute to multiple strategic governance activities.

1. Governance Documentation

The fellow will help develop and maintain governance documentation, including:

  • Governance frameworks
  • Committee charters
  • Terms of reference
  • Internal governance procedures
  • Organizational process documentation
  • Governance manuals

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Organizing governance document libraries
  • Maintaining version control
  • Ensuring accessibility of governance materials
  • Supporting preparation of governance documentation for meetings

2. Committee Coordination

The Governance Fellow will support Global Council committees by:

  • Coordinating meeting schedules
  • Preparing meeting agendas
  • Developing briefing materials
  • Recording meeting notes
  • Tracking action items
  • Supporting communication between committees and the Secretariat

This work ensures governance processes remain transparent, efficient, and well documented.

3. Democratic Transition Support

One of the fellowship’s major responsibilities involves supporting Global Artivism’s Democratic Transition Roadmap (2025–2027).

Tasks include:

  • Researching democratic governance models
  • Supporting electoral framework development
  • Preparing governance documentation
  • Tracking implementation milestones
  • Assisting with governance testing processes

4. Governance Research

The fellow will conduct research on governance models from:

  • Civil society organizations
  • Social movements
  • Cooperatives
  • Participatory organizations
  • Community-led governance systems

Research outputs may include:

  • Comparative analyses
  • Governance briefings
  • Policy notes
  • Knowledge products
  • Institutional recommendations

Qualifications

Applicants should demonstrate strong organizational, communication, and governance-related skills.

Required qualifications include:

  • Strong documentation and organizational skills
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines
  • Excellent written English
  • Independent working ability
  • Genuine interest in governance and institutional design
  • Commitment to Global Artivism’s mission and values
  • Ability to work as an independent contractor

Importantly, Global Artivism recognizes experience gained through:

  • Community organizing
  • Grassroots leadership
  • Cooperative governance
  • Participatory facilitation
  • Collective decision-making

These experiences are considered equally valuable alongside formal employment or academic credentials.

Preferred Qualifications

Although not mandatory, applicants with the following backgrounds are encouraged to apply:

  • Governance
  • Law
  • Political Science
  • International Development
  • Public Policy
  • Democracy Studies
  • Institutional Design

Experience with:

  • NGOs
  • Civil society organizations
  • Social movements
  • Arts organizations
  • Cultural institutions

will also strengthen an application.

Multilingual applicants—especially those speaking Global South languages—are strongly encouraged to apply.

Compensation

The Governance Fellow will receive a monthly contractor fee of:

USD 1,200–1,800 per month

Payments will be made upon submission of monthly invoices according to the contractor agreement.

Because this is an independent contractor engagement:

  • No employment salary is provided.
  • No employee benefits are included.
  • No paid leave is provided.
  • No insurance is provided by Global Artivism or Res Publica US Inc.
  • Contractors remain responsible for taxes, insurance, and legal compliance in their country of residence.

Benefits of Joining Global Artivism

Participants will gain access to valuable professional and leadership opportunities, including:

  • Monthly contractor compensation of USD 1,200–1,800
  • Flexible fully remote work
  • Structured mentorship
  • Practical governance experience
  • Collaboration with international governance experts
  • Participation in democratic institutional development
  • Exposure to international social justice movements
  • Opportunities for professional development
  • Access to global learning spaces
  • Networking with artists, activists, and cultural leaders worldwide
  • Experience working within a Global Majority-led movement

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

Global Artivism places particular emphasis on recruiting candidates from:

  • Global South countries
  • Global Majority communities
  • Grassroots organizations
  • Community movements
  • Cultural activism networks

The organization values lived experience equally alongside formal education and professional credentials.

Applicants with backgrounds in:

  • Community organizing
  • Grassroots leadership
  • Cultural work
  • Participatory governance
  • Movement building

are especially encouraged to submit expressions of interest.

Application Requirements

Interested candidates should prepare:

  • Completed online Expression of Interest
  • Personal information
  • Responses to motivation questions
  • Description of connections to Global South or Global Majority communities
  • Governance experience
  • Curriculum Vitae (PDF)

Applicants must also confirm their understanding of the independent contractor arrangement.

Application Timeline

  • Applications Open: Currently Open
  • Application Deadline: 16 July 2026 (10:00 PM MYT / UTC+8)
  • Shortlisted Candidates Contacted: By 22 July 2026

Applications submitted after the deadline will not be reviewed.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit an online Expression of Interest through the official Global Artivism application portal, ensuring all required questions are completed and supporting documents are uploaded before the deadline.

Applicants should carefully prepare thoughtful responses demonstrating their commitment to democratic governance, Global Majority leadership, and movement building.

Click HERE to apply!

Why You Should Apply

The Governance Fellow position offers far more than a typical remote contract.

Participants will become part of an international movement seeking to redefine governance through collaboration, justice, inclusion, and shared leadership. Fellows will contribute directly to institutional design while learning from experienced governance practitioners and movement leaders.

For emerging professionals interested in governance, democracy, public policy, institutional innovation, social justice, and international development, this fellowship provides an exceptional platform to strengthen both practical skills and global networks.

The fully remote nature of the role also enables participation regardless of geographic location, while Global Artivism’s commitment to Global South leadership ensures diverse perspectives remain central to its future.

Visit HERE to learn more about the Global Artivism Governance Fellow 2026.

Conclusion

The Global Artivism Governance Fellow 2026 presents a rare opportunity for emerging governance professionals, activists, researchers, and community leaders to contribute to the democratic evolution of a globally respected movement working at the intersection of arts, culture, governance, and social justice.

With competitive monthly compensation, flexible remote work, structured mentorship, and direct involvement in institutional governance and democratic transition, this fellowship is ideal for individuals committed to strengthening participatory leadership and building more just societies.

Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare their application materials early and submit their expression of interest before 16 July 2026 (10:00 PM MYT / UTC+8).

Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Global Artivism Governance Fellow 2026: Fully Remote Global Fellowship Prioritizing Global South Leaders | Earn USD 1,200–1,800 Monthly While Shaping Democratic Governance for a Global Arts and Social Justice Movement appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Women TechEU 2 EIC 2026–2027: Receive €75,000 in Non-Dilutive Funding for Women-Led Deep Tech Startups in Europe
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Women-led deep tech startups across Europe now have an exciting opportunity to accelerate their innovation and business growth through the Women TechEU 2 EIC programme. Funded by the European Union, this initiative is designed to empower women entrepreneurs by providing substantial financial support alongside personalized business development services. The programme offers €75,000 in non-dilutive funding […]

The post Women TechEU 2 EIC 2026–2027: Receive €75,000 in Non-Dilutive Funding for Women-Led Deep Tech Startups in Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Women-led deep tech startups across Europe now have an exciting opportunity to accelerate their innovation and business growth through the Women TechEU 2 EIC programme. Funded by the European Union, this initiative is designed to empower women entrepreneurs by providing substantial financial support alongside personalized business development services.

The programme offers €75,000 in non-dilutive funding to selected early-stage deep tech startups, ensuring that founders can scale their businesses without giving up equity. In addition to the grant, participants benefit from tailored mentoring, coaching, and training designed to strengthen leadership, innovation, commercialization, and business growth.

Applications for Women TechEU 2 EIC follow a two-stage application process, beginning with an Eligibility Strand before applicants can proceed to submit a Full Proposal.

This opportunity is ideal for women entrepreneurs leading innovative deep tech startups who are seeking funding, expert guidance, and access to a supportive European innovation ecosystem.

Application Period Opens: 1 June 2026

Final Eligibility Cut-off: 13 July 2027 (17:00 CEST, Brussels Time)

 

About Women TechEU 2 EIC

Women TechEU 2 EIC is a European Union-funded initiative that aims to strengthen the participation of women in Europe’s deep technology innovation ecosystem.

Recognizing the important role women entrepreneurs play in developing transformative technologies, the programme provides both financial resources and strategic business support to help startups overcome common barriers to growth.

By combining grant funding with business acceleration services, Women TechEU helps innovative startups move from early-stage development toward commercial success while strengthening Europe’s innovation landscape.

 

What Does the Programme Offer?

Selected startups receive a comprehensive support package designed to accelerate business growth.

1. €75,000 Non-Dilutive Funding

Successful applicants will receive:

  • €75,000 in grant funding.
  • No equity dilution.
  • Financial support to accelerate technology development and commercialization.

Because the funding is non-dilutive, founders retain full ownership of their companies while accessing critical growth capital.

 

2. Personalized Business Development Programme

Beyond funding, participants receive individualized support tailored to their business needs.

This includes:

  • One-on-one mentoring.
  • Professional coaching.
  • Business development training.
  • Strategic guidance.
  • Entrepreneurial capacity building.
  • Growth support.

The programme aims to strengthen both the technical and commercial capabilities of participating startups.

 

Two-Stage Application Process

Women TechEU 2 EIC uses a structured application process to ensure applicants meet the programme requirements before submitting a full proposal.

Stage 1: Eligibility Strand

The first step is the Eligibility Strand, which verifies whether applicants satisfy all participation requirements.

Only applicants who successfully pass this stage will be invited to submit a Full Proposal.

The Eligibility Strand helps streamline the evaluation process while ensuring only eligible startups proceed to the next stage.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to carefully review all application guidance before submitting their eligibility request.

 

Stage 2: Full Proposal

Applicants who successfully complete the Eligibility Strand will receive an invitation to submit a Full Proposal through the programme platform.

The Full Proposal will undergo a comprehensive evaluation before final funding decisions are made.

 

Eligibility Strand Timeline

The Women TechEU 2 EIC Eligibility Strand follows a rolling application schedule.

Opening Date

1 June 2026

Weekly Cut-Offs

Applications are accepted continuously with weekly cut-offs every Tuesday at 17:00 CE(S)T (Brussels time).

First Cut-Off

30 June 2026 – 17:00 CEST

Final Cut-Off

13 July 2027 – 17:00 CEST

Applicants are encouraged to submit their eligibility applications well before the desired cut-off date to allow sufficient time for processing.

 

When Will Applicants Receive Results?

Women TechEU has indicated that applicants will receive the outcome of their eligibility assessment through the Sploro Platform.

Results will be communicated no later than one week after the cut-off date under which the application was submitted.

 

Preparing Your Application

Applicants are strongly advised to carefully review all official application materials before submitting their eligibility request.

Key resources include:

  • Guidelines for Applicants
  • Application Form (Annex 1)
  • Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
  • Applicant Platform

Reviewing these documents carefully will help applicants understand the programme requirements and prepare a strong submission.

 

Women TechEU 2 EIC Open Call for Evaluators

In addition to supporting startups, Women TechEU is also recruiting independent evaluators to assess Full Proposal applications.

This opportunity is intended for qualified professionals with relevant expertise who can contribute to the programme’s evaluation process.

Evaluator Application Timeline

Opening Date: 15 June 2026

Closing Date: 30 July 2026 (15:00 CEST)

Interested evaluator applicants should carefully review the official Guidelines for Evaluators before applying.

Relevant documents include:

  • Evaluator Application Form
  • Guidelines for Evaluators
  • Evaluator Annex 1

Applicants will be selected based on their competencies and the requirements of the Full Proposal evaluation process.

 

Why Apply for Women TechEU?

Women TechEU provides much more than financial support.

Participants gain access to a comprehensive ecosystem that combines funding, mentoring, coaching, and business development support specifically tailored to women-led deep tech startups.

Key advantages include:

  • €75,000 non-dilutive funding.
  • Equity-free financial support.
  • Personalized mentoring.
  • Professional coaching.
  • Business development training.
  • Increased commercialization support.
  • Access to the European innovation ecosystem.
  • Opportunities to strengthen leadership and business strategy.

For women entrepreneurs seeking to scale innovative technologies, Women TechEU represents one of Europe’s leading support programmes dedicated specifically to women-led deep tech ventures.

 

Apply Early

Since eligibility applications are reviewed through weekly cut-off dates, prospective applicants should avoid waiting until the final deadline.

Submitting an application early provides more flexibility and allows sufficient time to address any issues before progressing to the Full Proposal stage.

Women-led deep tech startups that meet the programme requirements are encouraged to take advantage of this unique opportunity to access funding, mentorship, and strategic business support from the European Union.

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Grant opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Women TechEU 2 EIC 2026–2027: Receive €75,000 in Non-Dilutive Funding for Women-Led Deep Tech Startups in Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Research Officer (World Migration Report) – United Nations IOM Geneva: Job Opportunity Breakdown
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The International Organization for Migration (IOM), the leading UN agency in the field of migration, has announced a compelling professional opportunity for qualified candidates: Research Officer (World Migration Report), based in Geneva, Switzerland. This role sits within IOM Headquarters and is part of the organization’s competitive mobility exercise for internal and internal‑equivalent candidates. This article […]

The post Research Officer (World Migration Report) – United Nations IOM Geneva: Job Opportunity Breakdown appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Organization for Migration (IOM), the leading UN agency in the field of migration, has announced a compelling professional opportunity for qualified candidates: Research Officer (World Migration Report), based in Geneva, Switzerland. This role sits within IOM Headquarters and is part of the organization’s competitive mobility exercise for internal and internal‑equivalent candidates.

This article provides a comprehensive, SEO‑optimized overview of the position, responsibilities, qualifications, competencies, and application details to help potential applicants fully understand the opportunity.

About IOM and the World Migration Report

Established in 1951, IOM promotes humane and orderly migration worldwide. The organization works closely with governments, NGOs, and international partners to save lives, protect migrants, and strengthen migration governance. According to the document, “IOM is committed to fostering a respectful, inclusive and supportive workplace where all employees can thrive professionally and feel valued.”

The World Migration Report (WMR) is IOM’s flagship publication, offering authoritative insights into global migration trends, data, and thematic issues. The Research Officer plays a central role in shaping this influential report.

Position Overview

  • Job Title: Research Officer (World Migration Report)
  • Duty Station: Geneva, Switzerland
  • Contract Type: Fixed-term (1 year, with possibility of extension)
  • Initial Duration: 12 months
  • Grade: P‑2
  • Category: Job Opportunity
  • Application Deadline: 14 July 2026, 16:59 (Geneva local time)
  • Recruitment Type: Professional, internal mobility exercise

Key Responsibilities

The role is deeply embedded in research, editorial coordination, knowledge management, and global communication. As stated in the document, the officer will:

1. Contribute to the Development of the World Migration Report

  • Support the editorial team in designing, preparing, and coordinating the WMR series.
  • Work with internal and external contributors.
  • Draft key sections and co‑author thematic chapters.
  • Conduct data checking and provide additional research support. “Draft key parts of WMRs utilizing relevant data and other sources… participate in the data checking of WMR chapters.”

2. Enhance WMR Digital Tools

  • Contribute to updates of the WMR interactive platform and toolkits.

3. Support Global Dissemination and Outreach

  • Assist with WMR webpage updates, social media content, podcasts, info sheets, translations, media briefings, and launch events.

4. Strengthen Knowledge Management

  • Support development of online tools and webinar series.
  • Encourage use of WMR resources across HQ, Regional Offices, and Country Offices.

5. Provide Technical Guidance

  • Assist regional and country offices in designing and finalizing migration research outputs.

6. Produce High‑Quality Research Materials

  • Write, proofread, edit, and format factsheets, snapshots, articles, and reports.

7. Facilitate Internal Learning

  • Organize knowledge‑sharing activities to strengthen IOM’s research capacities.

8. Undertake Duty Travel

  • Travel as required for WMR promotion and dissemination.

Required Qualifications

Education

  • Master’s degree in Migration Studies, International Relations, Development Studies, Humanitarian Affairs, Political Science, or related fields with two years of experience, OR
  • Bachelor’s degree in the same fields with four years of experience.

Experience

  • Demonstrated experience writing migration‑related papers, briefings, and articles.
  • Experience conducting migration research and presenting findings.
  • Ability to explain complex migration data.
  • Experience collaborating with diverse teams and partners.

Skills

  • Excellent research, writing, analytical, and communication skills.
  • Strong drafting abilities and attention to detail.
  • Ability to work independently and under tight timelines.
  • Understanding of migration issues across geographic contexts.
  • Good computer literacy.

Languages

  • Fluency in English required.
  • Working knowledge of French and/or Spanish is an advantage.

IOM Core Values and Competencies

All IOM staff must demonstrate:

  • Inclusion and respect for diversity
  • Integrity and transparency
  • Professionalism
  • Courage
  • Empathy

Core competencies include teamwork, delivering results, managing knowledge, accountability, and communication.

Important Notes for Applicants

  • Internationally recruited staff must be mobile.
  • Appointment is subject to medical, security, and residency clearances.
  • IOM enforces a strict zero‑tolerance policy on misconduct, including sexual exploitation and abuse.
  • No fees are charged at any stage of recruitment.
  • Applications must be submitted through the IOM WAVE system.
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Deadline

Applications close on 14 July 2026 at 16:59 (Geneva local time).

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more job opportunities here

The post Research Officer (World Migration Report) – United Nations IOM Geneva: Job Opportunity Breakdown appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2027 Korea Foundation Support Program for Establishing Professorships in Korean Studies and Korean Language
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Korea Foundation (KF) has announced its 2027 Support for Establishment of Professorship program, designed to strengthen Korean Studies and Korean Language education at universities around the world. This initiative provides substantial financial assistance to institutions seeking to create new professorships, ensuring long-term academic growth and global engagement with Korea-related scholarship. Position (TTP)** The KF […]

The post 2027 Korea Foundation Support Program for Establishing Professorships in Korean Studies and Korean Language appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Korea Foundation (KF) has announced its 2027 Support for Establishment of Professorship program, designed to strengthen Korean Studies and Korean Language education at universities around the world. This initiative provides substantial financial assistance to institutions seeking to create new professorships, ensuring long-term academic growth and global engagement with Korea-related scholarship.

Position (TTP)** The KF covers up to 70% of the total salary and employee benefits for a newly hired professor in Korean Studies or Korean Language.

  • Salary: Based on the university’s existing salary scale.
  • Employee benefits: Includes legally required non-wage payments such as social welfare contributions and fringe benefits.

2. Endowed Chair Position

The KF provides annual endowment funding during the grant period to establish a permanent professorship. The university must contribute a 50% matching fund, dollar-for-dollar.

Grant Duration

  • Three to five years

Eligible Applicants

  • Universities outside Korea planning to establish new professorships in Korean Studies or Korean Language.

Prerequisites for KF Support

Universities must demonstrate the ability to sustain the professorship after KF support ends, either through internal funding or a jointly created endowment. Additionally, the appointed professor must undergo fair and rigorous evaluation according to the university’s standard procedures for appointment, promotion, and tenure.

Program Timeline

  1. Application Period: July 1, 2026 – September 11, 2026 Deadline: September 11, 2026 at 18:00 KST
  2. Notification of Results: December 2026
  3. Grant Start: 2027–28 or 2028–29 academic year

Required Documents

1. Online Application Form

Must include detailed information on the university’s current Korean Studies status (faculty, courses, library holdings, academic activities).

2. Supplementary Materials

The following documents must be uploaded:

  1. Curriculum for B.A./M.A. programs in Korean Studies or Korean Language (if applicable)
  2. University’s salary scale for professors
  3. CV of the proposed professor (if a candidate is already identified)
  4. CV of the project director
  5. Letter from the Dean supporting the establishment of the professorship

Evaluation Criteria

  1. Program Planning & Commitment
    • Clarity and organization of the proposal
    • Long-term plans for Korean Studies development
  2. Institutional Capacity
    • University reputation and prior achievements in Korean Studies
  3. Budget Soundness
    • Specificity and feasibility of the financial plan
    • Matching fund efforts
  4. Expected Outcomes
    • Contribution to regional diversification of Korean Studies
    • Practicality and impact of proposed goals

Important Reminders

  1. Cancellation of Support KF may cancel support if an agreement cannot be finalized with the university.
  2. Eligibility Requirements for Appointed Professors
    • At least 50% of teaching and research must focus on Korea
    • Research must use Korean-language primary sources
    • Publications must be in English, and preferably also in Korean
    • Korean language proficiency is required
  3. Notification of Changes Universities must promptly inform KF of any project changes.
  4. Revision Requests KF may request modifications or additions to the project plan.
  5. Grant Disbursement Funds are released after the grant agreement is signed and a qualified professor is hired.
  6. Annual Reporting Universities must submit yearly project reports with signed financial statements.

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more grant opportunities here 

The post 2027 Korea Foundation Support Program for Establishing Professorships in Korean Studies and Korean Language appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The Media Foundation for West Africa Opens Applications for the 9th Edition of the Next Generation Investigative Journalism Fellowship
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Media Foundation for West Africa (MFWA) has officially announced the call for applications for the 9th Edition of the Next Generation Investigative Journalism (NGIJ) Fellowship, a prestigious and fully funded three‑month residential program designed to nurture Ghana’s next wave of accountability and anti‑corruption journalists. The fellowship, scheduled to run from September to November 2026, […]

The post The Media Foundation for West Africa Opens Applications for the 9th Edition of the Next Generation Investigative Journalism Fellowship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Media Foundation for West Africa (MFWA) has officially announced the call for applications for the 9th Edition of the Next Generation Investigative Journalism (NGIJ) Fellowship, a prestigious and fully funded three‑month residential program designed to nurture Ghana’s next wave of accountability and anti‑corruption journalists. The fellowship, scheduled to run from September to November 2026, aims to strengthen investigative reporting, promote transparency, and enhance press freedom across Ghana.

Overview of the Fellowship

The NGIJ Fellowship is a flagship initiative under MFWA’s broader project titled “Countering corruption through accountability journalism and improved freedom of expression environment in Ghana.” This project forms part of the Participation, Accountability, Integrity for a Resilient Democracy (PAIReD) programme, commissioned by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), co‑financed by the European Union (EU) and the Swiss State Secretariat for Economic Affairs (SECO), and implemented by GIZ in cooperation with Ghana’s Ministry of Finance.

The overarching goal is to empower young journalists and media professionals with the skills, tools, and mentorship needed to produce impactful investigative stories that expose corruption, address socio‑economic inequalities, and strengthen democratic governance.

What Participants Will Gain

The fellowship offers a comprehensive blend of training, mentorship, and practical newsroom experience. Selected fellows will benefit from:

  1. Intensive Training Modules
    • Anti‑corruption and accountability reporting
    • Use of Ghana’s Right to Information (RTI) Law
    • Data journalism techniques
    • Fact‑checking methodologies
    • Additional investigative reporting tools and frameworks
  2. Hands‑On Mentorship Fellows will work directly with MFWA’s Independent Journalism Projects:
    • Fact‑Check Ghana
    • The Fourth Estate These platforms are known for producing high‑impact investigative stories and fact‑checking reports that shape national discourse.
  3. Story Production Support
    • Financial assistance to pursue investigative or in‑depth stories
    • Editorial guidance from seasoned investigative journalists
    • Opportunity to produce at least one major story and contribute to a collaborative group investigation
  4. Certification and Stipend
    • A Certificate of Honour upon successful completion
    • Monthly stipend throughout the three‑month residential program

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following criteria:

  1. Must be a journalism graduate, early‑career journalist, or young professional with a strong passion for journalism.
  2. Must commit fully to the residential program from September to November 2026.
  3. Female applicants and journalists residing outside the Greater Accra Region are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Process

 

  1. Motivation Letter (Maximum 400 words) explaining your interest in the fellowship.
  2. Two Published Articles
    • For journalists: print, TV, radio, or online articles
    • For non‑journalist professionals: a published blog post
  3. Letter of Consent from employer (for staff journalists).
  4. Curriculum Vitae (CV) with two referees.

Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

 

For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here

Discover more fellowship opportunities here

The post The Media Foundation for West Africa Opens Applications for the 9th Edition of the Next Generation Investigative Journalism Fellowship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The Horowitz Foundation for Social Policy: Comprehensive Guide to the 2024–2025 Grant Application Process
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Horowitz Foundation for Social Policy stands as a long‑established supporter of exceptional social policy research, offering competitive grants that empower scholars to pursue innovative, impactful projects. Since 1997, the Foundation has funded research that advances understanding of pressing social issues and contributes meaningfully to policy development. This detailed guide breaks down the full application […]

The post The Horowitz Foundation for Social Policy: Comprehensive Guide to the 2024–2025 Grant Application Process appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Horowitz Foundation for Social Policy stands as a long‑established supporter of exceptional social policy research, offering competitive grants that empower scholars to pursue innovative, impactful projects. Since 1997, the Foundation has funded research that advances understanding of pressing social issues and contributes meaningfully to policy development. This detailed guide breaks down the full application process, requirements, deadlines, and expectations to help applicants prepare a strong submission.

Overview of the Horowitz Foundation Grant Program

The Foundation provides financial support to emerging and established researchers whose work demonstrates originality, policy relevance, and academic rigor. Each year, applicants from diverse disciplines submit proposals addressing critical social challenges, ranging from inequality and governance to public health, education, and community development.

The grant program is administered entirely through an online portal, ensuring a streamlined and accessible application experience. Award recipients are announced in June each year, following a thorough review process conducted by the Foundation’s board and evaluators.

Application Steps: Full Breakdown

The application process consists of several structured components designed to evaluate both the researcher and the proposed project. Applicants must complete each step carefully to ensure eligibility.

1. Create an Account in the Application Portal

Applicants begin by clicking the Application Form button on the Foundation’s website. This leads to the secure application portal, where users must create an account. The portal allows applicants to save progress, upload documents, and track submission status.

2. Complete All Required Application Sections

The application includes multiple sections, each serving a specific purpose in evaluating the applicant’s qualifications and project potential.

A. Personal Details

This section consists of short‑answer questions that help reviewers understand the applicant’s background, academic trajectory, and motivations. Applicants should provide clear, concise responses that highlight relevant experience and interests.

B. Project Overview

A brief snapshot of the proposed research project. This overview should summarize the core idea, research question, and anticipated contribution to social policy.

C. Full Project Description (750 Words)

This is the most substantial part of the application. Applicants must submit a detailed description of their project, up to 750 words. Strong project descriptions typically include:

  • Research question and significance
  • Theoretical framework or conceptual foundation
  • Methodology and data sources
  • Expected outcomes and policy relevance
  • Timeline and feasibility

D. Upload Your CV

Applicants must upload a current curriculum vitae that outlines academic achievements, publications, research experience, and other relevant credentials.

E. Provide Contact Information for Recommenders

Applicants must list the contact details of recommenders who will submit formal recommendation forms. These individuals should be familiar with the applicant’s academic or professional work.

3. Review & Submit the Application

Once all sections are complete, applicants must click the Review & Submit button. This final step must be completed on or before the deadline: December 1 at 11:59 PM EST.

The portal allows applicants to review all entries before submission to ensure accuracy and completeness.

4. Ensure Recommenders Submit Their Forms

Recommendation forms must also be submitted by December 1 at 11:59 PM EST. Applications with missing recommendations will not be reviewed.

Applicants are encouraged to notify recommenders early and follow up as the deadline approaches.

Additional Important Information

To maintain fairness and consistency, the Foundation enforces several strict guidelines:

  • All applications must be submitted through the official online portal.
  • All forms and documents must be written in English.
  • Incomplete applications will not be considered.
  • Applications cannot be reviewed if recommendation forms are missing.
  • Award winners will be announced in June.
  • Applicants can join the Foundation’s email list to receive updates on deadlines, announcements, and opportunities.

 

For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here

Discover more  grant opportunities here

 

The post The Horowitz Foundation for Social Policy: Comprehensive Guide to the 2024–2025 Grant Application Process appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Project Proposals – UEFA Foundation for Children (2026 Edition)
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The UEFA Foundation for Children has officially opened its Call for Projects 2026, inviting organisations and NGOs across the globe to submit impactful initiatives that improve the lives of vulnerable children. This annual call, held throughout June and July, is one of the foundation’s most important mechanisms for advancing children’s rights, strengthening communities, and promoting […]

The post Call for Project Proposals – UEFA Foundation for Children (2026 Edition) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UEFA Foundation for Children has officially opened its Call for Projects 2026, inviting organisations and NGOs across the globe to submit impactful initiatives that improve the lives of vulnerable children. This annual call, held throughout June and July, is one of the foundation’s most important mechanisms for advancing children’s rights, strengthening communities, and promoting sustainable development through sport and social inclusion.

The foundation’s mission is rooted in solidarity, ethical values, and development aid. Its support extends far beyond Europe, embracing global organisations that share its commitment to protecting and empowering children.

Purpose of the Call for Projects

The UEFA Foundation for Children aims to identify and support innovative, practical, and ethically grounded projects that directly benefit children facing vulnerability, exclusion, or hardship. Through this initiative, the foundation promotes:

  • Fundamental rights of children
  • Access to safe environments
  • Social inclusion
  • Long-term community development

Organisations that apply must demonstrate a clear alignment with the foundation’s values and propose tangible solutions that create measurable impact.

Key Focus Areas

Applicants may submit projects that address one or more of the following priority areas:

  1. Access to Sport Projects that provide children with opportunities to participate in sports, regardless of background or ability.
  2. Personal Development Initiatives that support emotional, social, and educational growth.
  3. Disabled Children Programs designed to ensure inclusion, accessibility, and equal opportunities for children with disabilities.
  4. Healthy Lifestyle Activities promoting physical health, nutrition, and overall well-being.
  5. Conflict Victims Support for children affected by war, displacement, or violence.
  6. Gender Equality Projects that empower girls and promote equal access to opportunities.
  7. Environmental Protection Child-focused environmental education and sustainability initiatives.
  8. Employability Programs that equip older children and youth with skills for future employment.
  9. Infrastructure and Equipment Construction, renovation, or provision of safe spaces, sports facilities, or essential equipment.
  10. Strengthening Partnerships Collaborative initiatives that unite multiple organisations for greater impact.

Selection Process

Every submitted project undergoes a thorough evaluation by the UEFA Foundation for Children. The Board of Trustees reviews all proposals and makes final selections during its autumn meeting, following the foundation’s statutes and ethical guidelines.

Projects must meet strict criteria related to:

  • Ethical compliance
  • Feasibility
  • Sustainability
  • Impact measurement
  • Alignment with the foundation’s mission

Application Platform

The Candidate Registration Platform is now open for the 2026 Call for Projects. Eligible organisations are encouraged to prepare early and submit their proposals before the closing date.

Application Deadline: 3 August 2026.

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more grant  opportunities here

 

The post Call for Project Proposals – UEFA Foundation for Children (2026 Edition) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
